raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "A decline in enforcement of nonprofit status by the federal Internal Revenue Service has opened the door for for-profit colleges to use devious financial maneuvers to claim nonprofit status.",
    "Covert for-profit colleges will continue to mislead and prey on students without enforcement or oversight.",
    "Grand Canyon University reorganized and split off its real estate and academic-related assets in order to claim nonprofit status.",
    "Grand Canyon University contracted its for-profit company, Grand Canyon Education, Inc., to provide various services in exchange for about a 60% share of the revenue earned by the nonprofit college.",
    "The President of the nonprofit Grand Canyon University is also the CEO of the for-profit Grand Canyon Education, Inc.",
    "For-profit colleges are attempting to mislead consumers and regulators by claiming nonprofit and public status illicitly.",
    "Ashford University has stated it would follow Grand Canyon University's lead and claim nonprofit status.",
    "California Attorney General Xavier Becerra sued Ashford University for misrepresenting tuition fees and financial aid availability to prospective students.",
    "Investigations by the Chronicle of Higher Education have labeled Ashford University as a 'poster child for the ills of the for-profit college sector.'",
    "Using education as a tool to boost profits of owners constitutes a failure to support California students."
  ]
}
```
